source_name: User distilled premium web UI prompt set
content:
# User distilled premium web UI prompt set - Overview

Page type: component_pattern
Use cases: premium Liquid Glass system, glassmorphism landing page, AI product hero controls, floating navbar, glass CTA, premium card surfaces
Evidence: summary_only / high
Source: local-generated:2026-04-28/premium-web-ui-prompts

Visual traits:
- two-level glass material system
- subtle translucent cards and strong CTA glass
- gradient pseudo-element rim border
- inset highlight and controlled outer shadow
- blurred background media or ambient scenery visible through surfaces
- rounded full controls and rounded 2xl/3xl cards

Use when:
- premium Liquid Glass system
- glassmorphism landing page
- AI product hero controls
- floating navbar
- glass CTA
- premium card surfaces

Avoid when:
- ordinary dense admin table
- long text article
- strict high contrast workflow
- low-end heavy page
- no meaningful background behind glass
- hundreds of repeated glass cells

AI summary: Premium Liquid Glass implementation rules: use two glass levels, subtle translucent surfaces for nav/cards and strong glass for CTA/selected controls; add gradient pseudo-element borders, inset highlights, fallback colors, and contrast checks. For high-end landing pages and cinematic UI, Liquid Glass is a priority material; for dense CRUD/table tasks it remains optional.

source_name: User distilled premium web UI prompt set
content:
# User distilled premium web UI prompt set - Layout Rules

Page type: component_pattern
Use cases: premium Liquid Glass system, glassmorphism landing page, AI product hero controls, floating navbar, glass CTA, premium card surfaces
Evidence: summary_only / high
Source: local-generated:2026-04-28/premium-web-ui-prompts

- Use subtle glass for navbars, badges, chips, cards, and stat blocks.
- Use strong glass only for primary CTAs, emphasized controls, selected states, and hero surfaces.
- Keep strong glass away from dense data grids and hundreds of repeated rows.
- Glass needs meaningful background input; use media, ambient scenes, or gentle gradients behind it.
- Keep primary text on stable tinted glass surfaces and avoid busy detail under small text.

AI summary: Premium Liquid Glass implementation rules: use two glass levels, subtle translucent surfaces for nav/cards and strong glass for CTA/selected controls; add gradient pseudo-element borders, inset highlights, fallback colors, and contrast checks. For high-end landing pages and cinematic UI, Liquid Glass is a priority material; for dense CRUD/table tasks it remains optional.

content:
# User distilled premium web UI prompt set - Accessibility Notes

Page type: component_pattern
Use cases: premium Liquid Glass system, glassmorphism landing page, AI product hero controls, floating navbar, glass CTA, premium card surfaces
Evidence: summary_only / high
Source: local-generated:2026-04-28/premium-web-ui-prompts

- Provide backdrop-filter fallback colors.
- Check contrast after blur and media overlays are composited.
- Do not use color glow as the only state indicator.
- Respect reduced transparency where practical.
- Avoid low-contrast gray text on glass.

AI summary: Premium Liquid Glass implementation rules: use two glass levels, subtle translucent surfaces for nav/cards and strong glass for CTA/selected controls; add gradient pseudo-element borders, inset highlights, fallback colors, and contrast checks. For high-end landing pages and cinematic UI, Liquid Glass is a priority material; for dense CRUD/table tasks it remains optional.

source_name: User distilled premium web UI prompt set
content:
# User distilled premium web UI prompt set - Implementation Hints

Page type: component_pattern
Use cases: premium Liquid Glass system, glassmorphism landing page, AI product hero controls, floating navbar, glass CTA, premium card surfaces
Evidence: summary_only / high
Source: local-generated:2026-04-28/premium-web-ui-prompts

- Subtle glass background: rgba(255,255,255,0.01) to rgba(255,255,255,0.12).
- Subtle glass blur: backdrop-filter blur(4px) to blur(16px), with -webkit-backdrop-filter.
- Strong glass blur: blur(40px) to blur(50px), stronger inset highlight, subtle outer shadow, and gradient rim border.
- Use ::before with inset:0, border-radius:inherit, 1px-1.5px padding, linear-gradient rim, and mask-composite exclude/xor where supported.
- Use rounded-full for nav/badge/CTA and rounded-2xl or rounded-3xl for cards.
- Do not stop at bg-white/10 backdrop-blur; add rim, highlight, fallback, and controlled layering.

AI summary: Premium Liquid Glass implementation rules: use two glass levels, subtle translucent surfaces for nav/cards and strong glass for CTA/selected controls; add gradient pseudo-element borders, inset highlights, fallback colors, and contrast checks. For high-end landing pages and cinematic UI, Liquid Glass is a priority material; for dense CRUD/table tasks it remains optional.
